title: alangium villosum subsp. vitiense
intro: Alangium villosum subsp. vitiense, also known as the Fijian alangium or ki, is a species of flowering plant in the family Alangiaceae. It has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antibacterial, and antifungal properties. The health benefits of this plant include the treatment of various ailments such as fever, cough, and skin conditions, as well as its use as an antiseptic and wound healer. The plant can be prepared into herbal teas, infusions, and poultices, and is also used in traditional remedies for its purported ability to reduce fever and alleviate respiratory issues.
